interaction_phrase: At BQP, we apply quantum-inspired methods on existing CPUs and GPUs
occurrence_sentence: Nathan Mason: One of the biggest misconceptions is that organizations need quantum hardware before they can benefit from quantum technologies. At BQP, we apply quantum-inspired methods on existing CPUs and GPUs to improve optimization, simulation, and modeling workflows.

In this episode, Nate Mason from BQP discusses how quantum-inspired algorithms are already improving GPU efficiency, accelerating complex simulations, and helping organizations prepare for the transition toward quantum-ready infrastructure.

Founded with the vision of bringing quantum-inspired computing into real-world engineering environments, BQP develops technologies that help organizations solve computationally intensive problems using existing CPU, GPU, and HPC infrastructure. Its flagship platform, BQPhy®, combines quantum-inspired optimization, simulation, and digital twin capabilities to address bottlenecks in engineering workflows.

interaction_phrase: Member of the National Academy of Engineering
occurrence_sentence: "BQP's results signal the introduction of drastically higher computing power to flow field analysis and simulation. This capability can unlock new methods in aerospace development, enabling higher confidence during design and more proactive maintenance during the aircraft life cycle," said Dan Hart, Senior Aerospace Executive and Member of the National Academy of Engineering.
